stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x + ([[b]]) \). If \( f(f(x)) = [[k]] \), what is the value of \( x \)?
solution: Since \( f(f(x)) = [[a]]\big([[a]]x + ([[b]])\big) + ([[b]]) \), setting this equal to \( [[k]] \) and solving gives \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This solves the single equation \( f(x) = [[k]] \) instead of \( f(f(x)) = [[k]] \).
This negates the correct solution (a sign slip when isolating \( x \)).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Let \( g(u) = [[A]]u^{2} + ([[B]]) \) and \( f(x) = [[a]]x + ([[b]]) \). Which of the following is a solution of \( g(f(x)) = [[k]] \)?
solution: Since \( g(f(x)) = [[A]]\big([[a]]x+([[b]])\big)^{2} + ([[B]]) \), setting this equal to \( [[k]] \) gives \( \big([[a]]x+([[b]])\big)^{2} = \dfrac{[[k]]-([[B]])}{[[A]]} \). Taking the square root and solving, \( x = [[answer]] \) is one solution.
distractor_rationales: This forgets to square (or unsquare) properly, treating \( g \) as if it were linear in \( f(x) \).
This is the OTHER root's mirror value in the wrong direction, not an actual solution here.
This forgets to subtract off \( [[b]] \) before dividing by \( [[a]] \) after taking the square root.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by two from a correct solution.

stem: Let \( f(x) = [[af]]x + ([[bf]]) \), \( g(x) = [[c]]x + ([[d]]) \), and \( h(x) = [[e]]x + ([[m]]) \). Given that \( f(g(x)) = f(h(x)) \), what is \( x \)? (Hint: since \( f \) is one-to-one, you do not need to expand \( f \) at all.)
solution: Since \( f \) is one-to-one, \( f(g(x)) = f(h(x)) \) forces \( g(x) = h(x) \) directly (no need to ever expand \( f \)): \( [[c]]x + ([[d]]) = [[e]]x + ([[m]]) \), so \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the two slopes in the denominator instead of subtracting them.
This swaps the sign of both numerator and denominator, an inconsistent sign error that happens to look plausible.
This negates the correct value (a sign slip when isolating \( x \)).
This is off by one from the correct value.

This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]x + ([[b]]) \). Which of the following is a solution of \( \big(f(x)\big)^{2} = [[k]] \)?
solution: Taking the square root of both sides, \( f(x) = \pm\sqrt{[[k]]} \), so \( [[a]]x + ([[b]]) = \pm\sqrt{[[k]]} \). Solving the \( + \) case gives \( x = [[answer]] \) as one solution.
distractor_rationales: This subtracts \( b \) from \( k \) itself rather than from \( \pm\sqrt{k} \) before dividing by \( a \).
This reports \( \sqrt{k} \) directly without ever isolating \( x \) using \( f \)'s coefficients.
This divides by the wrong sign combination and omits the constant term \( b \) entirely.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by one from a correct solution.
This is off by two from a correct solution.

solution: Isolate the radical: \( \sqrt{x} = \dfrac{[[k]] - ([[b]])}{[[a]]} \), then square both sides: \( x = \left(\dfrac{[[k]]-([[b]])}{[[a]]}\right)^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This isolates \( \sqrt{x} \) correctly but forgets to square it to recover \( x \).
This squares correctly but then subtracts an extra, unneeded \( b \).
This negates the squared result, which cannot be correct since \( x \) must be non-negative.
